steps.title: Translate the year into x
steps.content: The graph uses $x$ for years after $2010$. The year $2010$ is $0$ years after $2010$, so use $x=0$.

steps.title: Read the model value
steps.content: At $x=0$, the graph has a marked point on the $y$-axis at $20{,}000$. This means the model estimates $20{,}000$ residents in $2010$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: $0$ is the input year offset, not the number of residents. The output at $x=0$ is $20,000$, so C is correct.
